title: Project Setup
description: Scaffold the .NET solution with clean architecture layers, SQLite persistence, Telegram presentation. Set up DI, configuration, and build pipeline.

title: Scaffold .NET solution and layer projects
description: Create solution, all classlib/webapi projects per clean arch convention: Core.Domain, Core.Application, Infrastructure.Persistence, Presentation.Telegram. Add project references following dependency direction. Add global.json pinning .NET 10.

title: Domain shared kernel (BaseEntity, CreatedEntity, MutableEntity)
description: Create SharedKernel base entity hierarchy in Core.Domain. BaseEntity (Guid Id), CreatedEntity (Timestamp), MutableEntity (CreatedAt, UpdatedAt).

title: SQLite persistence setup
description: Add EF Core SQLite packages to Infrastructure.Persistence. Create AppDbContext with SaveChangesAsync override for automatic timestamps. Configure SQLite connection string via IOptions pattern.

title: Generic repository and Unit of Work
description: Implement IRepository<T> in Core.Domain, Repository<T> in Infrastructure.Persistence. Implement IUnitOfWork in Core.Application, UnitOfWork in Infrastructure.Persistence.

title: Telegram bot host setup
description: Create Presentation.Telegram as a worker service. Add Telegram.Bot package. Configure bot token via IOptions. Set up polling or webhook receiver. Wire DI extensions from all layers in Program.cs.

title: DI registration extensions per layer
description: Each layer gets an Extensions/ folder with AddXxxServices extension method. Presentation.Telegram Program.cs chains them all.

title: Create Justfile with common dev commands
description: Add a Justfile with common development recipes: build, test, run, watch, clean, migrations, etc. Track it in git. Update as new layers/tools are added.

title: Integration test harness for Telegram layer
description:
Set up integration test infrastructure that tests the full system from the Telegram layer down, bypassing actual Telegram HTTP transport.

## Scope
- Add Presentation.Telegram project reference to Tests.Integration
- Create a unified UpdateHandler/dispatcher in the Telegram layer with HandleUpdateAsync(Update) entry point (coordinate with .5)
- Test fixture with full DI container: real SQLite in-memory DB, real services, fake/mock ITelegramBotClient
- Response capture mechanism — intercept bot SendMessageAsync, SendCallbackAnswerAsync, etc.
- Helper builders to construct Telegram Update objects (text messages, callback queries, inline keyboard presses)
- Base test class so future epics write tests like: SendText("/work") → assert response text/keyboard

## Acceptance Criteria
- A sample integration test that sends a text message through HandleUpdateAsync and asserts a response was captured
- All layers wired through real DI (no manual new-ing of services)
- SQLite in-memory so tests are fast and isolated
- Pattern is documented so subsequent epics follow it

title: Authentication & Registration
description: Private bot auth: BIP39 mnemonic registration flow, admin/user roles, first-user-is-admin, single-use mnemonics, ignore non-registered users.

title: User entity and admin flag
description: User entity in Core.Domain: Id, TelegramUserId, TelegramUsername, IsAdmin, UtcOffset, RegisteredAt. Repository interface.

title: BIP39 mnemonic generation
description: Service to generate 24-word BIP39 mnemonics using a Bitcoin/crypto library (NBitcoin). Store pending mnemonics in a static list. Single-use: remove after successful registration.

title: Registration flow
description: On startup with no users: log /register [mnemonic] to console. /register command in Telegram validates mnemonic, creates user. First user gets IsAdmin=true. Admin can generate additional mnemonics for other users.

title: Auth middleware — ignore non-users
description: Telegram update filter/middleware that checks if sender is a registered user. Non-users get ignored on all commands except /about. /about shows bot info and link to self-host.

title: Admin mnemonic management
description: Admin commands to generate new registration mnemonics (one or batch). List pending mnemonics. Revoke unused mnemonics.

title: Notifications
description: Take-lunch reminder (user sets hour), N-hours-worked alert, forgot-to-shutdown detection based on averages.

title: Take-lunch reminder
description: User configures a lunch reminder hour. If working past that hour without a lunch entry, bot sends a reminder.

title: Work-hours-complete alert
description: User sets target hours (e.g., 8h). Bot notifies when that threshold is reached for the day.

title: Forgot-to-shutdown detection
description: Based on user's average durations, detect when a state has been open unusually long and send a nudge notification.

title: User Settings
description: UTC offset on registration and editable later. Auto-shutdown of forgotten states (configurable per state: absolute time or percentage of normal like 130%).

title: UTC offset registration and editing
description: Prompt for UTC offset during registration. /settings command to view and change offset. Validate range (-12 to +14). Store on User entity.

title: Auto-shutdown configuration
description: Per-state auto-shutdown: if a tracking state exceeds configured threshold, automatically stop it. Configurable as absolute minutes or percentage of user's normal duration (e.g., 130%). Background job or check-on-next-command approach.

title: Base Time Tracking
description: Core tracking commands (/commute, /work, /lunch) with toggle logic, state machine, time offset parameters, and commute directionality. The fundamental feature of the bot.

title: Tracking state machine
description: Define tracking states: Idle, Commuting, Working, Lunch. State transitions enforce toggle/exclusive logic. Domain entity for TrackingSession (state, start, end, userId). State machine lives in Core.Domain.

title: Time entry entity and persistence
description: TimeEntry entity: Id, UserId, State (enum: Commute/Work/Lunch), StartedAt, EndedAt (nullable = active), CommuteDirection (ToWork/ToHome, nullable). Repository interface and EF configuration.

title: /commute /work /lunch command handlers
description: Telegram command handlers for /commute (/c), /work (/w), /lunch (/l). Parse optional params: -m/+m minute offset, [hh:mm] exact time. Call application service to start/stop states.

title: Tracking application service
description: Application service orchestrating state transitions. StartState(userId, state, timestamp): if active session exists and same state -> stop it (toggle). If different state -> stop previous, start new. Determine commute direction from context (first commute = to-work, after work = to-home).

title: Command parameter parsing
description: Parse optional parameters on tracking commands: -15/+15 for minute offset, [14:30] for exact 24h time. Return resolved UTC timestamp using user's offset. Shared parser in Application layer.

title: Alias registration (/c /w /l)
description: Register short aliases /c, /w, /l that route to the same handlers as /commute, /work, /lunch.

title: Simplify BaseEntity - remove unnecessary equality code
description: Remove IEquatable, Equals overrides, GetHashCode, and == \!= operators from BaseEntity. Just keep the Guid Id property and constructors. EF Core doesn't need this complexity and it's 1999-era C# boilerplate. Modern approach: entities are reference types, EF Core tracks by Id automatically.

title: Reports & Analytics
description: Average daily stats (work/commute/lunch), commute pattern analysis by direction and day of week, optimization suggestions, weekly/monthly/yearly aggregates.

title: Daily averages report
description: Report average daily worked hours, commute time (to-work and to-home separately), and lunch duration. Filterable by date range.

title: Commute pattern analysis
description: Analyze commute-to-work and commute-to-home patterns by day of week. Identify when commute is shortest. Suggest optimal departure times.

title: Weekly/monthly/yearly aggregates
description: Aggregate stats over week, month, year: total work hours, average commute, lunch patterns. Formatted as Telegram messages or simple text reports.

title: Editing & Corrections
description: /edit and /delete commands. Inline keyboard with time adjustment buttons (-30m -5m -1m +1m +5m +30m). Handle misclicks and bad entries.

title: /edit command with entry selection
description: /edit with no args edits last entry. /edit 1, /edit 2, /edit 3 selects N entries back. Show entry details and inline keyboard.

title: Inline keyboard time adjustments
description: Telegram inline keyboard with buttons: -30m -5m -1m +1m +5m +30m. Each click adjusts the entry's start time. Buttons can be clicked multiple times. Update entry in DB on each press.

title: /delete command with confirmation
description: /delete removes last entry (or /delete N for N-back). Show confirmation prompt (Yes/No inline keyboard) before deleting.
